content.text: I'll work through this audit systematically. Let me start by setting up a task list and orienting myself in the codebase.

todos.content: Step 1: Orient in the codebase - map project structure and central content model
Step 2: Map the Article contract and shared pieces it includes
Step 4: Audit every dependent of the Article contract by area
Step 5: Trace article identity and visibility guards
Step 6: Assess blast radius and rank risks

1: class Article < ApplicationRecord
2:   include LiquidEmbeddable
3:   include CloudinaryHelper
4:   include ActionView::Helpers
5:   include Reactable
6:   include Taggable
7:   include UserSubscriptionSourceable
8:   include PgSearch::Model
9:   include AlgoliaSearchable
10:   include WebpageTrackable
11: 
12:   acts_as_taggable_on :tags
13:   resourcify
14: 
15:   include StringAttributeCleaner.nullify_blanks_for(:canonical_url, on: :before_save)
16:   DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE = 18
17: 
18:   # When we cache an entity, either {User} or {Organization}, these are the names of the attributes
19:   # we cache.
20:   #
21:   # @note I would prefer that this constant were in the {Article::CachedEntity} namespace, but it
22:   #       didn't work out well.  Further, since Organization doesn't really know about
23:   #       Articles::CachedEntity, I'd rather it not "peek" into a class for which it has no
24:   #       knowledge.
25:   #
26:   # @note [@jeremyf] I have added the profile_image attribute, even though that's not one of the
27:   #       Articles::CachedEntity attributes.  This is necessary to detect the change.
28:   #
29:   # @see Articles::CachedEntity caching strategy for entity attributes
30:   ATTRIBUTES_CACHED_FOR_RELATED_ENTITY = %i[name profile_image profile_image_url slug username].freeze
31: 
32:   # admin_update was added as a hack to bypass published_at validation when admin is updating
33:   # TODO: [@lightalloy] remove published_at validation from the model and
34:   # move it to the services where the create/update takes place to avoid using hacks
35:   attr_accessor :publish_under_org, :admin_update
36:   attr_writer :series, :labels
37:   attr_accessor :body_url
38: 
39:   delegate :name, to: :user, prefix: true
40:   delegate :username, to: :user, prefix: true
41: 
42:   # touch: true was removed because when an article is updated, the associated collection
43:   # is touched along with all its articles(including this one). This causes eventually a deadlock.
44:   belongs_to :collection, optional: true
45: 
46:   belongs_to :organization, optional: true
47:   belongs_to :user
48:   belongs_to :subforem, optional: true
49: 
50:   counter_culture :user
51:   counter_culture :organization
52: 
53:   # The date that we began limiting the number of user mentions in an article.
54:   MAX_USER_MENTION_LIVE_AT = Time.utc(2021, 4, 7).freeze
55:   # all BIDI control marks (a part of them are expected to be removed during #normalize_title, but still)
56:   BIDI_CONTROL_CHARACTERS = /[\u061C\u200E\u200F\u202a-\u202e\u2066-\u2069]/
57: 
58:   MAX_TAG_LIST_SIZE = 4
59: 
60:   # Filter out anything that isn't a word, space, punctuation mark,
61:   # recognized emoji, and other auxiliary marks.
62:   # See: https://github.com/forem/forem/pull/16787#issuecomment-1062044359
63:   #
64:   # NOTE: try not to use hyphen (- U+002D) in comments inside regex,
65:   # otherwise it may break parser randomly.
66:   # Use underscore or Unicode hyphen (‐ U+2010) instead.
67:   # rubocop:disable Lint/DuplicateRegexpCharacterClassElement
68:   TITLE_CHARACTERS_ALLOWED = /[^
69:     [:word:]
70:     [:space:]
71:     [:punct:]
72:     \p{Sc}        # All currency symbols
73:     \u00a9        # Copyright symbol
74:     \u00ae        # Registered trademark symbol
75:     \u061c        # BIDI: Arabic letter mark
76:     \u180e        # Mongolian vowel separator
77:     \u200c        # Zero‐width non‐joiner, for complex scripts
78:     \u200d        # Zero-width joiner, for multipart emojis such as family
79:     \u200e-\u200f # BIDI: LTR and RTL mark (standalone)
80:     \u202c-\u202e # BIDI: POP, LTR, and RTL override
81:     \u2066-\u2069 # BIDI: LTR, RTL, FSI, and POP isolate
82:     \u20e3        # Combining enclosing keycap
83:     \u2122        # Trademark symbol
84:     \u2139        # Information symbol
85:     \u2194-\u2199 # Arrow symbols
86:     \u21a9-\u21aa # More arrows
87:     \u231a        # Watch emoji
88:     \u231b        # Hourglass emoji
89:     \u2328        # Keyboard emoji
90:     \u23cf        # Eject symbol
91:     \u23e9-\u23f3 # Various VCR‐actions emoji and clocks
92:     \u23f8-\u23fa # More VCR emoji
93:     \u24c2        # Blue circle with a white M in it
94:     \u25aa        # Black box
95:     \u25ab        # White box
96:     \u25b6        # VCR‐style play emoji
97:     \u25c0        # VCR‐style play backwards emoji
98:     \u25fb-\u25fe # More black and white squares
99:     \u2600-\u273f # Weather, zodiac, coffee, hazmat, cards, music, other misc emoji
100:     \u2744        # Snowflake emoji
101:     \u2747        # Sparkle emoji
102:     \u274c        # Cross mark
103:     \u274e        # Cross mark box
104:     \u2753-\u2755 # Big red and white ? emoji, big white ! emoji
105:     \u2757        # Big red ! emoji
106:     \u2763-\u2764 # Heart ! and heart emoji
107:     \u2795-\u2797 # Math operator emoji
108:     \u27a1        # Right arrow
109:     \u27b0        # One loop
110:     \u27bf        # Two loops
111:     \u2934        # Curved arrow pointing up to the right
112:     \u2935        # Curved arrow pointing down to the right
113:     \u2b00-\u2bff # More arrows, geometric shapes
114:     \u3030        # Squiggly line
115:     \u303d        # Either a line chart plummeting or the letter M, not sure
116:     \u3297        # Circled Ideograph Congratulation
117:     \u3299        # Circled Ideograph Secret
118:     \u{1f000}-\u{1ffff} # More common emoji
119:   ]+/m
120:   # rubocop:enable Lint/DuplicateRegexpCharacterClassElement

347:   # The trigger `update_reading_list_document` is used to keep the `articles.reading_list_document` column updated.
348:   #
349:   # Its body is inserted in a PostgreSQL trigger function and that joins the columns values
350:   # needed to search documents in the context of a "reading list".
351:   #
352:   # Please refer to https://github.com/jenseng/hair_trigger#usage in case you want to change or update the trigger.
353:   #
354:   # Additional information on how triggers work can be found in
355:   # => https://www.postgresql.org/docs/11/trigger-definition.html
356:   # => https://www.cybertec-postgresql.com/en/postgresql-how-to-write-a-trigger/
357:   #
358:   # Adapted from https://dba.stackexchange.com/a/289361/226575
359:   trigger
360:     .name(:update_reading_list_document).before(:insert, :update).for_each(:row)
361:     .declare("l_org_vector tsvector; l_user_vector tsvector") do
362:     <<~SQL
363:       NEW.reading_list_document :=
364:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.title, ''))), 'A') ||
365:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_tag_list, ''))), 'B') ||
366:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.body_markdown, ''))), 'C') ||
367:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_user_name, ''))), 'D') ||
368:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_user_username, ''))), 'D') ||
369:         setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig,
370:           unaccent(
371:             coalesce(
372:               array_to_string(
373:                 -- cached_organization is serialized to the DB as a YAML string, we extract only the name attribute
374:                 regexp_match(NEW.cached_organization, 'name: (.*)$', 'n'),
375:                 ' '
376:               ),
377:               ''
378:             )
379:           )
380:         ), 'D');
381:     SQL
382:   end

386:   serialize :cached_user, coder: YAML
387:

406:   # [@jgaskins] We use an index on `published`, but since it's a boolean value
407:   #   the Postgres query planner often skips it due to lack of diversity of the
408:   #   data in the column. However, since `published_at` is a *very* diverse
409:   #   column and can scope down the result set significantly, the query planner
410:   #   can make heavy use of it.

428:   scope :from_subforem, lambda { |subforem_id = nil|
429:     subforem_id ||= RequestStore.store[:subforem_id]

431:       # Includes articles with no subforem or subforem_id in Subforem.cached_discoverable_ids
432:       where("articles.subforem_id IN (?) OR articles.subforem_id IS NULL", [nil] + Subforem.cached_discoverable_ids)
433:     elsif [0, RequestStore.store[:default_subforem_id]].include?(subforem_id.to_i)
434:       where("articles.subforem_id IN (?) OR articles.subforem_id IS NULL", [nil, subforem_id, RequestStore.store[:default_subforem_id].to_i])
435:     else
436:       # where(subforem_id: subforem_id)
437:       where("articles.subforem_id = ?", subforem_id)
438:     end

520:   scope :sorting, lambda { |value|
521:     value ||= "creation-desc"
522:     kind, dir = value.split("-")
523: 
524:     dir = "desc" unless %w[asc desc].include?(dir)
525: 
526:     case kind
527:     when "creation"
528:       order(created_at: dir)
529:     when "views"
530:       order(page_views_count: dir)
531:     when "reactions"
532:       order(public_reactions_count: dir)
533:     when "comments"
534:       order(comments_count: dir)
535:     when "published"
536:       # NOTE: For recently published, we further filter to only published posts
537:       order(published_at: dir).published
538:     else
539:       order(created_at: dir)
540:     end
541:   }

596:   def self.seo_boostable(tag = nil, time_ago = 18.days.ago, limit: 20)
597:     # Time ago sometimes returns this phrase instead of a date
598:     time_ago = 5.days.ago if time_ago == "latest"
599: 
600:     # Time ago sometimes is given as nil and should then be the default. I know, sloppy.
601:     time_ago = 75.days.ago if time_ago.nil?
602: 
603:     relation = Article.published.from_subforem
604:       .order(organic_page_views_past_month_count: :desc)
605:       .where("score > ?", 8)
606:       .where("published_at > ?", time_ago)
607:       .limit(limit)
608: 
609:     fields = %i[path title comments_count created_at]
610:     if tag
611:       relation.cached_tagged_with(tag).pluck(*fields)
612:     else
613:       relation.pluck(*fields)
614:     end
615:   end

631:   def processed_html_final
632:     processed_html = replace_legacy_code_html(self.processed_html)
633: 
634:     return processed_html if ApplicationConfig["PRIOR_CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_DOMAIN"].blank? || ApplicationConfig["CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_DOMAIN"].blank?
635: 
636:     processed_html.gsub(ApplicationConfig["PRIOR_CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_DOMAIN"],
637:                         ApplicationConfig["CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_DOMAIN"])
638:   end
639: 
640:   def replace_legacy_code_html(html)
641:     return html if html.blank?
642:     return html unless html.include?("runkit-element")
643: 
644:     fragment = Nokogiri::HTML.fragment(html)
645:     fragment.css('.runkit-element').each do |element|
646:       preamble = element.at_css('code:nth-of-type(1)')&.text.to_s
647:       content = element.at_css('code:nth-of-type(2)')&.text.to_s
648: 
649:       replacement_html = LegacyCodeTag.fallback_html(
650:         preamble: preamble,
651:         parsed_content: content,
652:       )
653: 
654:       element.replace(Nokogiri::HTML.fragment(replacement_html))
655:     end
656: 
657:     fragment.to_html
658:   rescue StandardError
659:     html
660:   end
661:   private :replace_legacy_code_html

671:   def processed_description
672:     if body_text.present?

674:         .truncate(104, separator: " ")
675:         .tr("\n", " ")

682:   def title_finalized
683:     return title unless title.present?
684: 
685:     # First handle line breaks and paragraphs
686:     processed_title = title.dup
687: 
688:     # Convert double line breaks to paragraph breaks (with optional whitespace)
689:     processed_title.gsub!(/\n\s*\n/, "</p><p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">")
690: 
691:     # Convert single line breaks to <br> tags
692:     processed_title.gsub!("\n", "<br>")
693: 
694:     # Wrap the entire content in paragraph tags if we have any breaks
695:     if processed_title.include?("<br>") || processed_title.include?("</p><p")
696:       processed_title = "<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">#{processed_title}</p>"
697:       # Clean up any empty paragraphs that might have been created
698:       processed_title.gsub!("<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\"></p>", "")
699:       processed_title.gsub!(%r{<p class="quickie-paragraph">\s*</p>}, "")
700:       # Strip leading/trailing whitespace from paragraph content
701:       processed_title.gsub!(/<p class="quickie-paragraph">\s+/, "<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">")
702:       processed_title.gsub!(%r{\s+</p>}, "</p>")
703:     end
704: 
705:     # Regex to match URLs in the title
706:     url_regex = %r{https?://[^\s<>"{}|\\^`\[\]]+}
707: 
708:     # Replace each URL with a truncated, styled version
709:     processed_title.gsub(url_regex) do |url|
710:       # Strip protocol and www
711:       clean_url = url.gsub(%r{^https?://}, "").gsub(/^www\./, "")
712: 
713:       # Remove trailing slash
714:       clean_url = clean_url.chomp("/")
715: 
716:       # Truncate if longer than 25 chars
717:       display_url = if clean_url.length > 25
718:                       "#{clean_url[0..21]}..."
719:                     else
720:                       clean_url
721:                     end
722: 
723:       # Return HTML with styled span
724:       "<span style=\"text-decoration: underline;\">#{display_url}</span>"
725:     end.html_safe
726:   end
727: 
728:   def title_finalized_for_feed
729:     return title_finalized unless type_of == "status" && title.present?
730: 
731:     # Count total line breaks and paragraphs in the original title
732:     line_break_count = title.count("\n")
733: 
734:     # If we have 8 or more line breaks, truncate the title_finalized output
735:     if line_break_count >= 8
736:       truncate_title_for_feed
737:     else
738:       title_finalized
739:     end
740:   end
741: 
742:   def truncate_title_for_feed
743:     return title_finalized unless title.present?
744: 
745:     # Split the title into lines to find a good truncation point
746:     lines = title.split("\n")
747: 
748:     # Take first 6 lines (leaving room for "read more" indicator)
749:     truncated_lines = lines.first(6)
750: 
751:     # Reconstruct the truncated title
752:     truncated_title = truncated_lines.join("\n")
753: 
754:     # Process the truncated title through the same logic as title_finalized
755:     processed_title = truncated_title.dup
756: 
757:     # Convert double line breaks to paragraph breaks (with optional whitespace)
758:     processed_title.gsub!(/\n\s*\n/, "</p><p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">")
759: 
760:     # Convert single line breaks to <br> tags
761:     processed_title.gsub!("\n", "<br>")
762: 
763:     # Wrap the entire content in paragraph tags if we have any breaks
764:     if processed_title.include?("<br>") || processed_title.include?("</p><p")
765:       processed_title = "<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">#{processed_title}</p>"
766:       # Clean up any empty paragraphs that might have been created
767:       processed_title.gsub!("<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\"></p>", "")
768:       processed_title.gsub!(%r{<p class="quickie-paragraph">\s*</p>}, "")
769:       # Strip leading/trailing whitespace from paragraph content
770:       processed_title.gsub!(/<p class="quickie-paragraph">\s+/, "<p class=\"quickie-paragraph\">")
771:       processed_title.gsub!(%r{\s+</p>}, "</p>")
772:     end
773: 
774:     # Add "read more" indicator
775:     processed_title += '<span class="quickie-read-more">...read more</span>'
776: 
777:     # Handle URLs in the truncated content
778:     url_regex = %r{https?://[^\s<>"{}|\\^`\[\]]+}
779: 
780:     processed_title.gsub(url_regex) do |url|
781:       # Strip protocol and www
782:       clean_url = url.gsub(%r{^https?://}, "").gsub(/^www\./, "")
783: 
784:       # Remove trailing slash
785:       clean_url = clean_url.chomp("/")
786: 
787:       # Truncate if longer than 25 chars
788:       display_url = if clean_url.length > 25
789:                       "#{clean_url[0..21]}..."
790:                     else
791:                       clean_url
792:                     end
793: 
794:       # Return HTML with styled span
795:       "<span style=\"text-decoration: underline;\">#{display_url}</span>"
796:     end.html_safe
797:   end

939:     # Returns array of hashes with slug and organization info for UI display
940:     return [] unless user

945:     user.collections.where(organization_id: nil).each do |collection|
946:       series_list << {
947:         slug: collection.slug,
948:         organization_id: nil,
949:         organization_name: nil,
950:         is_personal: true
951:       }
952:     end

972:   def update_score
973:     base_subscriber_adjustment = user.base_subscriber? ? Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score : 0
974:     spam_adjustment = user.spam? ? -500 : 0
975:     negative_reaction_adjustment = Reaction.where(reactable_id: user_id, reactable_type: "User").sum(:points)
976: 
977:     user_featured_count_adjustment = 0
978:     featured_count = user.articles.featured.count
979:     user_featured_count_adjustment = ([featured_count, 10].min + Math.log(featured_count + 1)).to_i
980:     user_negative_count_adjustment = 0
981:     negative_count = user.articles.where("score < -10").count
982:     if negative_count.positive?
983:       user_negative_count_adjustment = -([negative_count,
984:                                           3].min + Math.log(negative_count + 1)).to_i
985:     end
986:     # Context notes are currently only a positive indicator. In the future, they could be negative and this should be changed.
987:     context_note_adjustment = context_notes.size
988: 
989:     # Content moderation label adjustments
990:     automod_label_adjustment = AUTOMOD_SCORE_ADJUSTMENTS[automod_label.to_sym] || 0
991: 
992:     badge_bonus_weight_sum = user.badges.sum(:bonus_weight)
993:     badge_reputation_bonus = Math.sqrt(badge_bonus_weight_sum).to_i
994: 
995:     organization_baseline_score = organization&.baseline_score || 0
996: 
997:     established_user_adjustment = (user.score.to_i > 100 && !clear_and_obvious_spam? && !likely_spam?) ? Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score.to_i : 0
998: 
999:     self.score = reactions.sum(:points) + spam_adjustment + negative_reaction_adjustment + base_subscriber_adjustment + user_featured_count_adjustment + user_negative_count_adjustment + context_note_adjustment + automod_label_adjustment + badge_reputation_bonus + organization_baseline_score + established_user_adjustment
1000:     accepted_max = [max_score, user&.max_score.to_i].min
1001:     accepted_max = [max_score, user&.max_score.to_i].max if accepted_max.zero?
1002:     self.score = accepted_max if accepted_max.positive? && accepted_max < score
1003: 
1004:     # Calculate comment_score and apply max_score limits
1005:     # Each comment can contribute a minimum of -1 to the total score
1006:     calculated_comment_score = comments.sum { |comment| [comment.score, -1].max }
1007:     comment_score = if accepted_max.positive? && accepted_max < calculated_comment_score
1008:                       accepted_max
1009:                     else
1010:                       calculated_comment_score
1011:                     end
1012: 
1013:     score_changed_flag = score_changed? || self.comment_score != comment_score
1014: 
1015:     update_columns(score: score,
1016:                    privileged_users_reaction_points_sum: reactions.privileged_category.sum(:points),
1017:                    comment_score: comment_score,
1018:                    hotness_score: BlackBox.article_hotness_score(self))
1019: 
1020:     trigger_linked_domain_score_updates if score_changed_flag
1021:     trigger_freeform_context_note_generation
1022:     trigger_summary_generation
1023:     trigger_semantic_embedding_generation if score_changed_flag
1024:   end
1025:

1161:   def generate_video_embed_url
1162:     return if video_source_url.blank?
1163:

1165:       begin
1166:         self.video = YoutubeParser.new(video_source_url).call
1167:         p "Parsed YouTube video URL: #{video}" if Rails.env.development?
1168:       rescue StandardError => e
1169:         Rails.logger.error("Error parsing YouTube video URL: #{e.message}")
1170:       end
1171:     elsif video_source_url.include?("player.mux.com")
1172:       begin
1173:         parser = MuxParser.new(video_source_url)
1174:         self.video = parser.call
1175:         self.video_thumbnail_url = mux_thumbnail_url(parser.video_id) if parser.video_id
1176:         p "Parsed Mux video URL: #{video}" if Rails.env.development?
1177:       rescue StandardError => e
1178:         Rails.logger.error("Error parsing Mux video URL: #{e.message}")
1179:       end
1180:     elsif video_source_url.include?("twitch.tv")
1181:       begin
1182:         parser = TwitchParser.new(video_source_url)
1183:         self.video = parser.call
1184:         p "Parsed Twitch video URL: #{video}" if Rails.env.development?
1185:       rescue StandardError => e
1186:         Rails.logger.error("Error parsing Twitch video URL: #{e.message}")
1187:       end
1188:     end
1189:   end

1191:   def extract_url_from_status_title
1192:     return unless status? && title.present? && body_url.blank?
1193:

1198:     url_match = title.match(url_pattern)
1199:     return unless url_match
1200:

1202:     # Remove trailing punctuation that might not be part of the URL
1203:     extracted_url = extracted_url.sub(/[.,;:!?)]+$/, "")

1222:     # This prevents duplicating embed tags that were added by add_urls_from_title_to_body
1223:     embed_tag = "{% embed #{body_url} minimal %}"

1301:     elsif title.gsub(/\p{Space}+/u, '').length > max_length

1311:   def restrict_attributes_with_status_types

1316:     # Check if the value actually changed, not just if Rails marked it as changed
1317:     if persisted? && body_markdown_changed? && body_markdown_was != body_markdown
1318:       errors.add(:body_markdown,
1319:                  "cannot be modified for status type posts. Consider unpublishing if you need to make changes.")
1320:       return
1321:     end
1322: 
1323:     # Return early if this is already saved and the body_markdown hasn't changed
1324:     return if persisted?
1325: 
1326:     # For new status types, allow body_markdown only if it contains embed tags from URLs in title
1327:     if body_url.blank? && body_markdown.present?
1328:       # Allow body_markdown if it only contains embed tags that were added from URLs in title
1329:       unless body_markdown_only_contains_embed_tags_from_title?
1330:         errors.add(:body_markdown, "is not allowed for status types")
1331:       end
1332:     elsif body_url.blank? && (main_image.present? || collection_id.present?)

1384:   def fetch_video_duration
1385:     return if video_source_url&.include?("youtube.com") || video_source_url&.include?("player.mux.com")
1386: 
1387:     if video.present? && video_duration_in_seconds.zero?
1388:       url = video_source_url.gsub(".m3u8", "1351620000001-200015_hls_v4.m3u8")
1389:       duration = 0
1390:       HTTParty.get(url).body.split("#EXTINF:").each do |chunk|
1391:         duration += chunk.split(",")[0].to_f
1392:       end
1393:       self.video_duration_in_seconds = duration
1394:       duration
1395:     end
1396:   rescue StandardError => e
1397:     Rails.logger.error(e)
1398:   end

1430:     set_main_image(hash)

1432: 
1433:     update_description = hash["description"].present? || hash["title"].present?

1447:     elsif hash.key?("cover_image")
1448:       # They've chosen the set cover image in the front matter, so we'll proceed with that assumption.

1459:   # When an article is saved, it ensures that the tags that were adjusted by moderators and admins
1460:   # remain adjusted. We do not allow the author to add or remove tags that were previously added or
1461:   # removed by moderators and admins.
1462:   #
1463:   # This method is called before validation, so that the tag_list can be validated.
1464:   #
1465:   # @return [String] an array of tag names.
1466:   def validate_tag
1467:     distinct_tag_adjustments = TagAdjustment.where(article_id: id, status: "committed")
1468:       .select('DISTINCT ON ("tag_id") *')
1469:       .order(:tag_id, updated_at: :desc, id: :desc)
1470: 
1471:     remove_tag_adjustments_from_tag_list(distinct_tag_adjustments)
1472:     add_tag_adjustments_to_tag_list(distinct_tag_adjustments)
1473: 
1474:     # check there are not too many tags
1475:     return errors.add(:tag_list, I18n.t("models.article.too_many_tags")) if tag_list.size > MAX_TAG_LIST_SIZE
1476: 
1477:     validate_tag_name(tag_list)
1478:   end

1480:   def remove_tag_adjustments_from_tag_list(distinct_adjustments)
1481:     tags_to_remove = distinct_adjustments.select { |adj| adj.adjustment_type == "removal" }.pluck(:tag_name)
1482:     tag_list.remove(tags_to_remove, parse: true) if tags_to_remove.present?
1483:   end
1484: 
1485:   def add_tag_adjustments_to_tag_list(distinct_adjustments)
1486:     tags_to_add = distinct_adjustments.select { |adj| adj.adjustment_type == "addition" }.pluck(:tag_name)
1487:     return if tags_to_add.blank?
1488: 
1489:     tag_list.add(tags_to_add, parse: true)
1490:     self.tag_list = tag_list.map { |tag| Tag.find_preferred_alias_for(tag) }
1491:   end

1553:   def future_or_current_published_at

1560:   def correct_published_at?
1561:     return true unless changes["published_at"]
1562: 
1563:     # for drafts (that were never published before) or scheduled articles
1564:     # => allow future or current dates, or no published_at
1565:     if !published_at_was || published_at_was > Time.current
1566:       # for articles published_from_feed (exported from rss) we allow past published_at
1567:       if (published_at && published_at < 15.minutes.ago) && !published_from_feed
1568:         errors.add(:published_at, I18n.t("models.article.future_or_current_published_at"))
1569:       end
1570:     else
1571:       # for articles that have been published already (published or unpublished drafts) => immutable published_at
1572:       # allow changes within one minute in case of editing via frontmatter w/o specifying seconds
1573:       has_nils = changes["published_at"].include?(nil) # changes from nil or to nil
1574:       close_enough = !has_nils && (published_at_was - published_at).between?(-60, 60)
1575:       errors.add(:published_at, I18n.t("models.article.immutable_published_at")) if has_nils || !close_enough
1576:     end
1577:   end

1789:     self.body_markdown = if body_markdown.present?

1796:   def extract_urls_from_title
1797:     return [] unless title.present?
1798: 
1799:     # Regex to match URLs in the title
1800:     # This regex matches http/https URLs, including those with query parameters and fragments
1801:     url_regex = %r{https?://[^\s<>"{}|\\^`\[\]]+}
1802: 
1803:     urls = title.scan(url_regex).uniq
1804:     # Remove trailing punctuation that might not be part of the URL
1805:     urls.map { |url| url.sub(/[.,;:!?)]+$/, "") }
1806:   end

1824:     urls_from_title = extract_urls_from_title
1825:     return false if urls_from_title.empty?
1826: 
1827:     # Create the expected embed tags
1828:     expected_embed_tags = urls_from_title.map { |url| "{% embed #{url} minimal %}" }
1829: 
1830:     # Clean the body_markdown (remove extra whitespace and newlines)
1831:     cleaned_body = body_markdown.strip.squeeze("\n")
1832: 
1833:     # Check if the body_markdown contains only the expected embed tags
1834:     # Allow for some whitespace variations
1835:     expected_content = expected_embed_tags.join("\n")
1836: 
1837:     # Normalize both strings for comparison
1838:     normalized_body = cleaned_body.gsub(/\s+/, " ").strip
1839:     normalized_expected = expected_content.gsub(/\s+/, " ").strip
1840: 
1841:     normalized_body == normalized_expected

15:       (respond_to?(:saved_change_to_published?) && saved_change_to_published?) ||
16:       (respond_to?(:saved_change_to_published_at?) && saved_change_to_published_at?)

19:   def sync_liquid_embed_metadata

23:       published_at: respond_to?(:published_at) ? published_at : updated_at
24:     )

13:   class_methods do
14:     # Batch sync reaction counts for multiple records efficiently
15:     # Uses transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
16:     # Eliminates N+1 queries by using a single grouped query + batch update
17:     #
18:     # @param records [Array<ActiveRecord::Base>] records to sync
19:     # @return [Integer] number of records updated
20:     def sync_reactions_count_for_batch(records)
21:       return 0 if records.empty?
22: 
23:       ids = records.map(&:id)
24: 
25:       transaction do
26:         # Lock the rows to prevent concurrent updates (SKIP LOCKED for non-blocking)
27:         where(id: ids).lock("FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED").load
28: 
29:         # Single grouped query to get correct counts for all records
30:         correct_counts = Reaction
31:           .where(reactable_type: name, reactable_id: ids)
32:           .public_category
33:           .group(:reactable_id)
34:           .count
35: 
36:         # Use Arel for safe SQL generation instead of string interpolation
37:         update_cases = ids.map do |record_id|
38:           count = correct_counts[record_id] || 0
39:           Arel.sql("WHEN #{connection.quote(record_id)} THEN #{count.to_i}")
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Perform batch update using ActiveRecord with sanitized Arel
43:         where(id: ids).update_all(
44:           Arel.sql(
45:             "public_reactions_count = CASE id #{update_cases.join(' ')} END",
46:           ),
47:         )
48:       end
49: 
50:       ids.size
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   def sync_reactions_count
55:     # Use transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
56:     self.class.transaction do
57:       self.class.where(id: id).lock("FOR UPDATE").load
58:       correct_count = reactions.public_category.count
59:       self.class.where(id: id).update_all(public_reactions_count: correct_count)
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Thread-safe sync that can be called from background jobs
64:   def sync_reactions_count!
65:     sync_reactions_count
66:     reload
67:   end
68: 
69:   def public_reaction_categories
70:     # Get reaction counts for this reactable
71:     reaction_counts = reaction_counts_for_reactable
72: 
73:     # Filter to only public reaction categories that have reactions
74:     categories_with_counts = reaction_counts.select do |count_data|
75:       category_slug = count_data[:category]
76:       count_data[:count].positive? && ReactionCategory[category_slug]&.visible_to_public?
77:     end
78: 
79:     # Sort by count (descending), then by position for ties, and limit to 3
80:     sorted_categories = categories_with_counts
81:       .sort_by { |count_data| [-count_data[:count], ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]&.position || 99] }
82:       .first(3)
83: 
84:     # Convert back to ReactionCategory objects
85:     sorted_categories.map do |count_data|
86:       ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   private
91: 
92:   def reaction_counts_for_reactable
93:     return [] unless respond_to?(:id)
94: 
95:     Rails.cache.fetch("reaction_counts_for_reactable-#{self.class.name}-#{id}", expires_in: 10.hours) do
96:       reactions.group(:category).count.map do |category, count|
97:         { category: category, count: count }
98:       end
99:     end
100:   end
101: end

1: module Taggable
2:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
3: 
4:   # rubocop:disable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
5:   included do
6:     scope :cached_tagged_with, lambda { |tag|
7:       case tag
8:       when String, Symbol
9:         if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
10:           # Accelerate native `WHERE` filtering escaping sequential regex scans by leveraging GIN index bounds organically dynamically. 
11:           where("tags_array @> ARRAY[?]::text[]", tag.to_s)
12:         else
13:           # In Postgres regexes, the [[:<:]] and [[:>:]] are equivalent to "start of
14:           # word" and "end of word", respectively. They're similar to `\b` in Perl-
15:           # compatible regexes (PCRE), but that matches at either end of a word.
16:           # They're more comparable to how vim's `\<` and `\>` work.
17:           where("cached_tag_list ~ ?", "[[:<:]]#{tag}[[:>:]]")
18:         end
19:       when Array
20:         tag.reduce(self) { |acc, elem| acc.cached_tagged_with(elem) }
21:       when Tag
22:         cached_tagged_with(tag.name)
23:       else
24:         raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tag.inspect}"
25:       end
26:     }
27: 
28:     scope :cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
29:       case tags
30:       when String, Symbol
31:         cached_tagged_with(tags)
32:       when Array
33:         if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
34:           # Accelerate `any` conditions natively utilizing absolute overlap intersections eliminating `OR` sequential bottlenecks dynamically!
35:           where("tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", tags.map(&:to_s))
36:         else
37:           tags
38:             .map { |tag| cached_tagged_with(tag) }
39:             .reduce { |acc, elem| acc.or(elem) }
40:         end
41:       when Tag
42:         cached_tagged_with(tags.name)
43:       else
44:         raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tags.inspect}"
45:       end
46:     }
47: 
48:     # We usually try to avoid using Arel directly like this. However, none of the more
49:     # straight-forward ways of negating the above scope worked:
50:     # 1. A subquery doesn't work because we're not dealing with a simple NOT IN scenario.
51:     # 2. where.not(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).where_values_hash) doesn't work because where_values_hash
52:     #    only works for simple conditions and returns an empty hash in this case.
53:     scope :not_cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
54:       where(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).arel.constraints.reduce(:or).not)
55:     }
56: 
57:     before_save :sync_tags_array
58: 
59:     def sync_tags_array
60:       return unless self.class.column_names.include?("tags_array")
61:       
62:       # We meticulously avoid N+1 evaluation queries by identifying if ActsAsTaggableOn
63:       # tags were explicitly interrogated/mutated. 
64:       # Forem intrinsically instantiates `@tag_list` when validating front matter tags.
65:       tags_were_mutated = cached_tag_list_changed? ||
66:                           instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list) ||
67:                           (cached_tag_list.present? && tags_array.blank?)
68:       
69:       return unless tags_were_mutated
70:       
71:       new_array = if instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list)
72:                     tag_list.to_a
73:                   else
74:                     cached_tag_list.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:blank?).compact
75:                   end
76:                   
77:       self.tags_array = new_array unless tags_array == new_array
78:     end
79:   end
80:   # rubocop:enable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
81: 
82:   # we check tags names aren't too long and don't contain non alphabet characters
83:   def validate_tag_name(tag_list)
84:     tag_list.each do |tag|
85:       new_tag = Tag.new(name: tag)
86:       new_tag.validate_name
87:       new_tag.errors.messages[:name].each { |message| errors.add(:tag, "\"#{tag}\" #{message}") }
88:     end
89:   end
90: end

1: class ApplicationRecord < ActiveRecord::Base
2:   self.abstract_class = true
3: 
4:   include Purgeable
5: 
6:   # see <https://www.postgresql.org/docs/11/catalog-pg-class.html> for details
7:   # on the `pg_class` table
8:   QUERY_ESTIMATED_COUNT = <<~SQL.squish.freeze
9:     SELECT (
10:       (reltuples / GREATEST(relpages, 1)) *
11:       (pg_relation_size(?) / (GREATEST(current_setting('block_size')::integer, 1)))
12:     )::bigint AS count
13:     FROM pg_class
14:     WHERE relname = ? AND relkind = 'r';
15:   SQL
16: 
17:   # Computes an estimated count of the number of rows using stats collected by VACUUM
18:   # inspired by <https://www.citusdata.com/blog/2016/10/12/count-performance/#dup_counts_estimated_full>
19:   # and <https://stackoverflow.com/a/48391562/4186181>
20:   def self.estimated_count
21:     query = sanitize_sql_array([QUERY_ESTIMATED_COUNT, table_name, table_name])
22:     result = connection.execute(query)
23: 
24:     count = result.first["count"]
25:     result.clear # PG::Result is manually managed in memory, we need to release its resources
26:     count
27:   end
28: 
29:   # Decorate collection with appropriate decorator
30:   def self.decorate
31:     decorator_class.decorate_collection(all)
32:   end
33: 
34:   # Infers the decorator class to be used by (e.g. `User` maps to `UserDecorator`).
35:   # adapted from https://github.com/drapergem/draper/blob/157eb955072a941e6455e0121fca09a989fcbc21/lib/draper/decoratable.rb#L71
36:   def self.decorator_class(called_on = self)
37:     prefix = respond_to?(:model_name) ? model_name : name
38:     decorator_name = "#{prefix}Decorator"
39:     decorator_name_constant = decorator_name.safe_constantize
40:     return decorator_name_constant unless decorator_name_constant.nil?
41: 
42:     return superclass.decorator_class(called_on) if superclass.respond_to?(:decorator_class)
43: 
44:     raise UninferrableDecoratorError,
45:           I18n.t("models.application_record.uninferrable", class: called_on.class.name)
46:   end
47: 
48:   def self.statement_timeout
49:     connection.execute("SELECT setting FROM pg_settings WHERE name = 'statement_timeout'")
50:       .first["setting"]
51:       .to_i
52:       .seconds / 1000
53:   end
54: 
55:   def self.with_statement_timeout(duration, connection: self.connection)
56:     original_timeout = statement_timeout
57:     milliseconds = (duration.to_f * 1000).to_i
58:     connection.execute "SET statement_timeout = #{milliseconds}"
59:     yield
60:   ensure
61:     milliseconds = original_timeout.to_i * 1000
62:     connection.execute "SET statement_timeout = #{milliseconds}"
63:   end
64: 
65:   # Used for high-volume inserts to prevent unnecessary locking and blocking of other operations
66:   def self.with_synchronous_commit_off
67:     transaction do
68:       connection.execute("SET LOCAL synchronous_commit TO OFF")
69:       yield
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   # ActiveRecord's `find_each` method allows you to work with a large collection of records
74:   # in batches, but strictly only orders those batches by IDs in ascending order.
75:   # Any other specified order is either ignored or raises an error (depending on configuration).
76:   # This method allows performing batch queries of arbitrary order.
77:   #
78:   # @param batch_size [Integer] Batch size limit
79:   # @yieldparam [self]
80:   # @return [Enumerator<self>] if no block is given
81:   #
82:   # @see https://api.rubyonrails.org/v7.0.4.2/classes/ActiveRecord/Batches.html#method-i-find_each
83:   def self.find_each_respecting_scope(batch_size: 1000, &block)
84:     load_in_batches = Enumerator.new do |e|
85:       in_batches_respecting_scope(batch_size: batch_size) do |batch|
86:         batch.each { |record| e.yield record }
87:       end
88:     end
89: 
90:     return load_in_batches unless block
91: 
92:     load_in_batches.each(&block)
93:   end
94: 
95:   def self.in_batches_respecting_scope(batch_size: 1000)
96:     relation = self
97: 
98:     # Without a specified order, the sorting of PostgreSQL's query results is undefined behaviour
99:     relation = relation.order(id: :asc) if all.arel.orders.blank?
100:     all_ids = relation.ids.to_a
101: 
102:     # `where` and `order` are unnecessary as we already know the exact records we need (and in what order)
103:     # `limit` and `offset` would conflict with the manual batching
104:     batch_relation = relation.unscope(:where, :order, :limit, :offset)
105:     # We're loading in batches to reduce memory usage; if the results get cached anyway, that defeats the purpose
106:     batch_relation.skip_query_cache!
107: 
108:     all_ids.in_groups_of(batch_size, false) do |ids|
109:       records = batch_relation.where(id: ids).index_by(&:id)
110:       # Avoid yielding nil if e.g. record has been deleted since loading IDs
111:       yield ids.filter_map { |id| records[id] }
112:     end
113:   end
114: 
115:   # Decorate object with appropriate decorator
116:   def decorate
117:     self.class.decorator_class.new(self)
118:   end
119: 
120:   def decorated?
121:     false
122:   end
123: 
124:   # In our view objects, we often ask "What's this object's class's name?"
125:   #
126:   # We can either first check "Are you decorated?"  If so, ask for the decorated object's class
127:   # name.  Or we can add a helper method for that very thing.
128:   #
129:   # @return [String]
130:   def class_name
131:     self.class.name
132:   end
133: 
134:   def errors_as_sentence
135:     errors.full_messages.to_sentence
136:   end
137: end

1: module Articles
2:   module Destroyer
3:     module_function
4: 
5:     def call(article)
6:       author = article.user
7: 
8:       # comments will automatically lose the connection to their article once `.destroy` is called,
9:       # due to the `dependent: nullify` clause, so to remove their notifications,
10:       # we need to cache the ids in advance
11:       article_comments_ids = article.comments.ids
12: 
13:       article.destroy!
14: 
15:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article")
16: 
17:       unless article_comments_ids.blank?
18:         Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article_comments_ids, notifiable_type: "Comment")
19:       end
20: 
21:       EdgeCache::BustUser.call(author)
22:     end
23:   end
24: end

21:     def initialize(user, article_params)

23:       @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)

26:     def call

28:

40:     def normalize_params(original_params)
41:       original_params.except(:tags).tap do |params|
42:         # convert tags from array to a string
43:         if (tags = original_params[:tags]).present?
44:           params[:tag_list] = tags.join(", ")
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     def rate_limit!
50:       rate_limit_to_use = if user.decorate.considered_new?
51:                             :published_article_antispam_creation
52:                           else
53:                             :published_article_creation
54:                           end
55: 
56:       user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(rate_limit_to_use)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def refresh_auto_audience_segments
60:       user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
61:     end
62: 
63:     def complete_onboarding_first_post
64:       return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
65: 
66:       user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
67:     end
68: 
69:     def create_article
70:       begin
71:         @article = Article.new(article_params) do |article|
72:           article.user_id = user.id
73:           article.show_comments = true
74:         end
75:       rescue ArgumentError => e
76:         if e.message.include?("is not a valid type_of")
77:           @article = Article.new(article_params.except(:type_of)) do |article|
78:             article.user_id = user.id
79:           end
80:           @article.errors.add(:type_of, :invalid)
81:           return @article
82:         else
83:           raise e
84:         end
85:       end
86: 
87:       # Set collection after creation to avoid it being cleared by evaluate_front_matter
88:       # which clears collection_id when title is present in frontmatter
89:       if @article.save
90:         found_series = series
91:         if found_series.present?
92:           @article.update_column(:collection_id, found_series.id)
93:           @article.association(:collection).reset
94:         end
95:       end
96:       @article
97:     end
98: 
99:     def series
100:       @series ||= if article_params[:series].blank?
101:                     nil
102:                   else
103:                     organization = article_params[:organization_id].present? ? Organization.find_by(id: article_params[:organization_id]) : nil
104:                     Collection.find_series(article_params[:series], user, organization: organization)
105:                   end
106:     end
107: 
108:     # Subscribe author to notifications for all comments on their article.
109:     def subscribe_author
110:       NotificationSubscription.create(user: user,
111:                                       notifiable: article,
112:                                       config: "all_comments")
113:     end
114:   end
115: end

1: module Articles
2:   class Builder
3:     LINE_BREAK = "\n".freeze
4: 
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(user, tag, prefill)
10:       @user = user
11:       @tag = tag
12:       @prefill = prefill
13: 
14:       @editor_version2 = @user&.setting&.editor_version == "v2"
15:     end
16: 
17:     # the Builder returns a pair of [article, needs_authorization?]
18:     # => `needs_authorization? can be either true or false
19:     def call
20:       return [tag_user_editor_v2, true] if tag && editor_version2
21:       return [tag_user, true] if tag&.submission_template.present? && user
22:       return [prefill_user_editor_v2, true] if prefill.present? && editor_version2
23:       return [prefill_user, true] if prefill.present? && user
24:       return [tag_article, false] if tag
25:       return [user_editor_v2, false] if editor_version2
26: 
27:       [user_editor_v1, false]
28:     end
29: 
30:     private
31: 
32:     attr_reader :user, :tag, :prefill, :editor_version2
33: 
34:     def tag_user_editor_v2
35:       submission_template = tag.submission_template_customized(user.name).to_s
36: 
37:       Article.new(
38:         body_markdown: submission_template.split("---").last.to_s.strip,
39:         cached_tag_list: tag.name,
40:         processed_html: "",
41:         user_id: user.id,
42:         title: normalized_text(submission_template, "title:"),
43:       )
44:     end
45: 
46:     def tag_user
47:       Article.new(
48:         body_markdown: tag.submission_template_customized(user.name),
49:         processed_html: "",
50:         user_id: user.id,
51:       )
52:     end
53: 
54:     def prefill_user_editor_v2
55:       Article.new(
56:         body_markdown: prefill.split("---").last.to_s.strip,
57:         cached_tag_list: normalized_text(prefill, "tags:"),
58:         processed_html: "",
59:         user_id: user.id,
60:         title: normalized_text(prefill, "title:"),
61:       )
62:     end
63: 
64:     def prefill_user
65:       Article.new(
66:         body_markdown: prefill,
67:         processed_html: "",
68:         user_id: user.id,
69:       )
70:     end
71: 
72:     def tag_article
73:       Article.new(
74:         body_markdown: "---\ntitle: \npublished: false\ndescription: \ntags: #{tag.name}\n---\n\n",
75:         processed_html: "",
76:         user_id: user&.id,
77:       )
78:     end
79: 
80:     def user_editor_v2
81:       Article.new(user_id: user.id)
82:     end
83: 
84:     def user_editor_v1
85:       published_at = Time.current.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %z")
86:       published_at_str = FeatureFlag.enabled?(:schedule_articles) ? "# published_at: #{published_at}\n" : ""
87:       body = "---\ntitle: \npublished: false\ndescription: " \
88:              "\ntags: \n# cover_image: https://direct_url_to_image.jpg" \
89:              "\n# Use a ratio of 100:42 for best results.\n#{published_at_str}---\n\n"
90: 
91:       Article.new(
92:         body_markdown: body,
93:         processed_html: "",
94:         user_id: user&.id,
95:       )
96:     end
97: 
98:     def normalized_text(source, split_pattern)
99:       text = source.split(split_pattern).second.to_s
100:       text.split(LINE_BREAK).first.to_s.strip
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

<content>
1: module Mentions
2:   # This class creates mentions + associated notifications for Articles and Comments.
3:   # This class will check to see if there are any @-mentions in the post, and will
4:   # create the associated mentions inline if necessary.
5:   class CreateAll
6:     def self.call(...)
7:       new(...).call
8:     end
9: 
10:     def initialize(notifiable)
11:       @notifiable = notifiable
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call
15:       mentioned_users = users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
16: 
17:       delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(mentioned_users)
18:       create_mentions_for(mentioned_users)
19:     end
20: 
21:     private
22: 
23:     def users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
24:       mentioned_usernames = extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
25: 
26:       collect_existing_users(mentioned_usernames)
27:         .then do |existing_mentioned_users|
28:           reject_notifiable_author(existing_mentioned_users)
29:         end
30:     end
31: 
32:     def collect_existing_users(usernames)
33:       User.registered.where(username: usernames)
34:     end
35: 
36:     def create_mentions_for(users)
37:       users.each { |user| create_mention_for(user) }
38:     end
39: 
40:     def extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
41:       # The "mentioned-user" css is added by Html::Parser#user_link_if_exists
42:       doc = Nokogiri::HTML(notifiable.processed_html)
43: 
44:       # Remove any mentions that are embedded within any liquid tags
45:       non_liquid_tag_mentions = doc.css(".mentioned-user").reject do |tag|
46:         tag.ancestors(".liquid-comment").any? ||
47:           tag.ancestors("[class^=ltag]").any? ||
48:           tag.ancestors("[class*=' ltag']").any?
49:       end
50: 
51:       non_liquid_tag_mentions.map { |link| link.text.delete("@").downcase }
52:     end
53: 
54:     def reject_notifiable_author(users)
55:       users.reject { |user| authored_by?(user, notifiable) }
56:     end
57: 
58:     def authored_by?(user, notifiable)
59:       user.id == notifiable.user_id
60:     end
61: 
62:     def delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(users)
63:       mentions = notifiable.mentions.where.not(user_id: users).destroy_all
64:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: mentions.map(&:id), notifiable_type: "Mention") if mentions.present?
65:     end
66: 
67:     def user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
68:       user.notifications.exists?(notifiable_id: notifiable.id, notifiable_type: "Comment")
69:     end
70: 
71:     def create_mention_for(user)
72:       # Do not create additional notifications for being mentioned in a comment.
73:       if notifiable.is_a?(Comment) && user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
74:         return
75:       end
76: 
77:       # The mentionable_type is the model that created the mention, the user is the user to be mentioned.
78:       mention = Mention.create(user_id: user.id, mentionable_id: notifiable.id,
79:                                mentionable_type: notifiable.class.name)
80: 
81:       # If notifiable is an Article, we need to create the notification for the mention immediately so
82:       # that the notification exists in the database before we attempt to create other Article-related notifications.
83:       # However, if notifiable is a Comment, we can create the notification for the mention in the background.
84:       case notifiable
85:       when Article
86:         Notification.send_mention_notification_without_delay(mention)
87:       when Comment
88:         Notification.send_mention_notification(mention)
89:       end
90: 
91:       mention
92:     end
93: 
94:     attr_reader :notifiable
95:   end
96: end

(End of file - total 96 lines)

<content>
1: module Articles
2:   class Updater
3:     Result = Struct.new(:success, :article, keyword_init: true)
4: 
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(user, article, article_params)
10:       @user = user
11:       @article = article
12:       @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
13:     end
14: 
15:     def call
16:       user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(:article_update)
17:       success = article.update(article_params)
18: 
19:       if success
20:         user.rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:article_update)
21: 
22:         if became_unpublished?
23:           remove_all_notifications
24:           bust_author_profile_cache
25:         end
26: 
27:         send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers if remains_published?
28:         refresh_auto_audience_segments if became_published?
29:         complete_onboarding_first_post if became_published?
30:       end
31: 
32:       Result.new(success: success, article: article.decorate)
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     attr_reader :user, :article, :article_params
38: 
39:     def normalize_params(original_params)
40:       article_params = original_params.dup
41: 
42:       # updated edited time only if already published and not edited by an admin
43:       update_edited_at = article.user == user && article.published
44: 
45:       # remove published_at values received from a user if an articles was published before (has past published_at)
46:       # published_at will remain as it was in this case
47:       article_params.delete :published_at if article.published_at && !article.scheduled?
48: 
49:       # NOTE: It's surprising that this is article.user and not @user
50:       Articles::Attributes.new(article_params, article.user, organization: article.organization)
51:         .for_update(update_edited_at: update_edited_at)
52:     end
53: 
54:     def refresh_auto_audience_segments
55:       user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
56:     end
57: 
58:     def complete_onboarding_first_post
59:       return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
60: 
61:       user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
62:     end
63: 
64:     def became_published?
65:       article.published? && !article.published_previously_was
66:     end
67: 
68:     def remains_published?
69:       article.published && article.saved_change_to_published.blank?
70:     end
71: 
72:     def became_unpublished?
73:       article.saved_changes["published"] == [true, false]
74:     end
75: 
76:     # If the article has already been published and is only being updated, then we need to create
77:     # mentions and send notifications to mentioned users inline via the Mentions::CreateAll service.
78:     def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers
79:       Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)
80:     end
81: 
82:     # Remove any associated notifications if Article is unpublished
83:     def remove_all_notifications
84:       Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article",
85:                                                       action: "Published")
86:       ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article",
87:                                     action: "Published")
88: 
89:       if article.comments.exists?
90:         Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids,
91:                                 notifiable_type: "Comment")
92:       end
93:       return unless article.mentions.exists?
94: 
95:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.mentions.ids,
96:                               notifiable_type: "Mention")
97:     end
98: 
99:     def bust_author_profile_cache
100:       EdgeCache::BustUser.call(article.user)
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

(End of file - total 103 lines)

1: module Notifications
2:   class Update
3:     delegate :article_data, :comment_data, :user_data, :organization_data, to: Notifications
4: 
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(notifiable, action = nil)
10:       @notifiable = notifiable
11:       @action = action
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call
15:       return unless [Article, Comment].include?(notifiable.class)
16: 
17:       notifications = Notification.where(
18:         notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
19:         notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
20:         action: action,
21:       )
22:       # as we only select the first notification right after, there is no need
23:       # to load all of them in memory with `.blank?`, thus we choose `.none?`
24:       return if notifications.none?
25: 
26:       new_json_data = {}
27:       new_json_data[notifiable.class.name.downcase] = public_send("#{notifiable.class.name.downcase}_data", notifiable)
28:       new_json_data[:user] = user_data(notifiable.user)
29:       add_organization_data = notifiable.is_a?(Article) && notifiable.organization
30:       new_json_data[:organization] = organization_data(notifiable.organization) if add_organization_data
31: 
32:       notifications.update_all(json_data: new_json_data)
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     attr_reader :notifiable, :action
38:   end
39: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # send notification about the action ("Published") that happened on a notifiable (Article)
2: module Notifications
3:   module NotifiableAction
4:     FOLLOWER_SEND_LIMIT = 10_000
5:     class Send
6:       def self.call(...)
7:         new(...).call
8:       end
9: 
10:       # @param notifiable [Article]
11:       # @param action [String] for now only "Published"
12:       def initialize(notifiable, action = nil)
13:         @notifiable = notifiable
14:         @action = action
15:       end
16: 
17:       delegate :user_data, :article_data, :organization_data, to: Notifications
18: 
19:       def call
20:         return unless notifiable.is_a?(Article)
21: 
22:         json_data = {
23:           user: user_data(notifiable.user),
24:           article: article_data(notifiable)
25:         }
26:         json_data[:organization] = organization_data(notifiable.organization) if notifiable.organization_id
27: 
28:         notifications_attributes = []
29: 
30:         # If a user was mentioned in the article, they will have already received a mention.
31:         # We explicitly need to exclude them from the article_followers array if they already
32:         # have a mention in order to avoid sending a user multiple notifications for one article.
33:         user_ids_with_article_mentions = notifiable.mentions&.pluck(:user_id)
34: 
35:         article_followers = User.joins("INNER JOIN follows ON follows.follower_id = users.id")
36:           .where("(follows.followable_id = ? AND follows.followable_type = ?)
37:                  OR (follows.followable_id = ? AND follows.followable_type = ?)",
38:                  notifiable&.user&.id, "User", notifiable&.organization&.id, "Organization")
39:           .where(follows: { subscription_status: "all_articles" })
40:           .where.not(id: (user_ids_with_article_mentions + [notifiable.user]))
41:           .recently_active(FOLLOWER_SEND_LIMIT).distinct
42: 
43:         article_followers.find_each do |follower|
44:           now = Time.current
45:           notifications_attributes.push(
46:             user_id: follower.id,
47:             notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
48:             notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
49:             subforem_id: notifiable.subforem_id,
50:             action: action,
51:             json_data: json_data,
52:             created_at: now,
53:             notified_at: now,
54:             updated_at: now,
55:           )
56:         end
57: 
58:         return if notifications_attributes.blank?
59: 
60:         upsert_index = choose_upsert_index(action)
61: 
62:         context_notification_attributes = {
63:           context_id: notifiable.id,
64:           context_type: notifiable.class.name,
65:           action: action
66:         }
67: 
68:         ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
69:           Notification.upsert_all(
70:             notifications_attributes,
71:             unique_by: upsert_index,
72:             returning: %i[id],
73:           )
74: 
75:           ContextNotification.upsert(context_notification_attributes,
76:                                      unique_by: :index_context_notification_on_context_and_action)
77:         end
78:       end
79: 
80:       private
81: 
82:       attr_reader :notifiable, :action
83: 
84:       def choose_upsert_index(action)
85:         return :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_and_action_not_null if action.present?
86: 
87:         :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_action_is_null
88:       end
89:     end
90:   end
91: end

(End of file - total 91 lines)

1: module Notifications
2:   module NewMention
3:     class Send
4:       include ActionView::Helpers::TextHelper
5: 
6:       delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
7:       delegate :comment_data, to: Notifications
8:       delegate :article_data, to: Notifications
9: 
10:       def self.call(...)
11:         new(...).call
12:       end
13: 
14:       def initialize(mention)
15:         @mention = mention
16:       end
17: 
18:       def call
19:         return if mention.mentionable.score.negative?
20: 
21:         Notification.create(
22:           user_id: mention.user_id,
23:           notifiable_id: mention.id,
24:           notifiable_type: "Mention",
25:           subforem_id: mention.mentionable.subforem_id,
26:           action: nil,
27:           json_data: json_data,
28:         )
29: 
30:         # Send PNs using Rpush - respecting users' notificaton delivery settings
31:         return unless Users::NotificationSetting.find_by(user_id: mention.user_id)&.mobile_mention_notifications?
32: 
33:         target = mention.user_id
34:         message_key, mentionable_title =
35:           if mention.mentionable.is_a?(Article)
36:             ["views.notifications.mention.article_mobile", mention.mentionable.title.strip]
37:           else
38:             ["views.notifications.mention.comment_mobile", mention.mentionable.commentable.title.strip]
39:           end
40: 
41:         PushNotifications::Send.call(
42:           user_ids: [target],
43:           title: I18n.t("services.notifications.new_mention.new"),
44:           body: "#{I18n.t(
45:             message_key,
46:             user: mention.mentionable.user.username,
47:             title: mentionable_title, # For an article this should be title, for comment should be article's title
48:           )}:\n" \
49:               "#{strip_tags(mention.mentionable.processed_html).strip}",
50:           payload: {
51:             url: URL.url(mention.mentionable.path),
52:             type: "new mention"
53:           },
54:         )
55:       end
56: 
57:       private
58: 
59:       attr_reader :mention
60: 
61:       def json_data
62:         data = { user: user_data(mention.mentionable.user) }
63: 
64:         case mention.mentionable_type
65:         when "Comment"
66:           data[:comment] = comment_data(mention.mentionable)
67:         when "Article"
68:           data[:article] = article_data(mention.mentionable)
69:         end
70: 
71:         data
72:       end
73:     end
74:   end
75: end

1: # Send notifications from moderation
2: module Notifications
3:   module Moderation
4:     MODERATORS_AVAILABILITY_DELAY = 22.hours
5:     class Send
6:       SUPPORTED = [Comment, Article].freeze
7: 
8:       def self.call(...)
9:         new(...).call
10:       end
11: 
12:       def initialize(moderator, notifiable)
13:         @moderator = moderator
14:         @notifiable = notifiable
15:       end
16: 
17:       delegate :user_data, :comment_data, :article_data, to: Notifications
18: 
19:       def call
20:         return unless notifiable_supported?(notifiable)
21: 
22:         # do not create the notification if the comment/article was created by
23:         # the moderator of the user has the `limited` role
24:         return if notifiable.user.limited? || moderator == notifiable.user
25: 
26:         json_data = { user: user_data(User.staff_account) }
27:         notifiable_name = notifiable.class.name.downcase
28:         json_data[notifiable_name] = public_send "#{notifiable_name}_data", notifiable
29:         json_data["#{notifiable_name}_user"] = user_data(notifiable.user)
30:         new_notification = Notification.create!(

1: module Articles
2:   class HandleSpamWorker
3:     include Sidekiq::Job
4: 
5:     sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority
6: 
7:     def perform(article_id)
8:       article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
9:       return unless article
10: 
11:       # Always run spam handling and score update
12:       Spam::Handler.handle_article!(article: article)
13:       article.reload.update_score
14: 
15:       # Only enhance if clickbait score is 0 (not already processed) and score is >= 0 (good quality after spam check)
16:       return unless article.clickbait_score.zero? && article.score >= 0
17: 
18:       enhance_article(article)
19:     end
20: 
21:     private
22: 
23:     def enhance_article(article)
24:       # Create AI client for dependency injection
25:       ai_client = Ai::Base.new
26:       enhancer = Ai::ArticleEnhancer.new(article, ai_client: ai_client)
27: 
28:       # Calculate and update clickbait score
29:       clickbait_score = enhancer.calculate_clickbait_score
30:       article.update_column(:clickbait_score, clickbait_score)
31: 
32:       # Generate and apply tags if article has no tags and meets criteria
33:       if should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
34:         suggested_tags = enhancer.generate_tags
35:         apply_tags_to_article(article, suggested_tags) if suggested_tags.any?
36:       end
37:     rescue StandardError => e
38:       Rails.logger.error("Article enhancement failed for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
39:     end
40: 
41:     def should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
42:       article.cached_tag_list.blank? &&
43:         article.score >= 0 &&
44:         clickbait_score < 0.6
45:     end
46: 
47:     def apply_tags_to_article(article, tag_names)
48:       # Validate that all suggested tags exist and are supported
49:       valid_tags = Tag.supported.where(name: tag_names).pluck(:name)
50: 
51:       if valid_tags.any?
52:         # Apply tags to article
53:         article.tag_list = valid_tags
54:         article.cached_tag_list = valid_tags.join(", ")
55:         article.save
56: 
57:         Rails.logger.info("Applied tags #{valid_tags.join(', ')} to article #{article.id}")
58:       else
59:         Rails.logger.warn("No valid tags found from suggestions #{tag_names.join(', ')} for article #{article.id}")
60:       end
61:     end
62:   end
63: end

1: module UserSubscriptionSourceable
2:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
3: 
4:   # This all assumes there's an association with User under the column user_id.
5: 
6:   included do
7:     has_many :user_subscriptions, as: :user_subscription_sourceable, dependent: :nullify
8:     has_many :sourced_subscribers,
9:              class_name: "User",
10:              through: :user_subscriptions,
11:              source: :subscriber,
12:              foreign_key: :user_id
13:   end
14: 
15:   def build_user_subscription(subscriber)
16:     UserSubscription.new(user_subscription_attributes(subscriber))

1: # Copied from the deprecated fastly-rails gem
2: # https://github.com/fastly/fastly-rails/blob/master/lib/fastly-rails/active_record/surrogate_key.rb
3: #
4: # This concern handles purge and purge_all calls to purge Fastly's edge cache.
5: # If Fastly has not been configured, these methods will short circuit and not be invoked.
6: module Purgeable
7:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
8: 
9:   module ClassMethods
10:     def purge_all
11:       return unless fastly
12: 
13:       service.purge_by_key(table_key)
14:     end
15: 
16:     def soft_purge_all
17:       return unless fastly
18: 
19:       service.purge_by_key(table_key, true)
20:     end
21: 
22:     def table_key
23:       table_name
24:     end
25: 
26:     def fastly
27:       return false if Rails.env.development?
28:       return false if ApplicationConfig["FASTLY_API_KEY"].blank? || ApplicationConfig["FASTLY_SERVICE_ID"].blank?
29: 
30:       Fastly.new(api_key: ApplicationConfig["FASTLY_API_KEY"])
31:     end
32: 
33:     def service
34:       return unless fastly
35: 
36:       Fastly::Service.new({ id: ApplicationConfig["FASTLY_SERVICE_ID"] }, fastly)
37:     end
38:   end
39: 
40:   # Instance methods
41:   def record_key
42:     "#{table_key}/#{id}"
43:   end
44: 
45:   def table_key
46:     self.class.table_key
47:   end
48: 
49:   def purge
50:     return unless fastly
51: 
52:     service.purge_by_key(record_key)
53:   end
54: 
55:   def soft_purge
56:     return unless fastly
57: 
58:     service.purge_by_key(record_key, true)
59:   end
60: 
61:   def purge_all
62:     self.class.purge_all
63:   end
64: 
65:   def soft_purge_all
66:     self.class.soft_purge_all
67:   end
68: 
69:   def fastly
70:     self.class.fastly
71:   end
72: 
73:   def service
74:     self.class.service
75:   end
76: end

31:     subforem_id ||= RequestStore.store[:subforem_id]
32:     if subforem_id.present? && subforem_id == RequestStore.store[:root_subforem_id]
33:       # No additional conditions; just return the current scope
34:       where(nil)
35:     elsif [0, RequestStore.store[:default_subforem_id]].include?(subforem_id.to_i)
36:       where("notifications.subforem_id IN (?) OR notifications.subforem_id IS NULL", [nil, subforem_id, RequestStore.store[:default_subforem_id].to_i])
37:     else
38:       where("notifications.subforem_id = ?", subforem_id)

42:   class << self
43:     def send_new_follower_notification(follow, is_read: false)
44:       return unless follow && Follow.need_new_follower_notification_for?(follow.followable_type)
45:       return if follow.followable_type == "User" && UserBlock.blocking?(follow.followable_id, follow.follower_id)
46: 
47:       follow_data = Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData.coerce(follow).to_h
48:       follower = User.find_by(id: follow.follower_id)
49:       if follower.registered_at > 48.hours.ago # Delay the job 60 minutes to check for spam users if new user
50:         Notifications::NewFollowerWorker.perform_in(1.hour, follow_data, is_read)
51:       else
52:         Notifications::NewFollowerWorker.perform_async(follow_data, is_read)
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def send_new_follower_notification_without_delay(follow, is_read: false)
57:       return unless follow && Follow.need_new_follower_notification_for?(follow.followable_type)
58:       return if follow.followable_type == "User" && UserBlock.blocking?(follow.followable_id, follow.follower_id)
59: 
60:       follow_data = Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData.coerce(follow).to_h
61:       Notifications::NewFollowerWorker.new.perform(follow_data, is_read)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers(notifiable, _action = nil)
65:       return unless notifiable.is_a?(Article) && notifiable.published? && notifiable.type_of == "full_post"
66: 
67:       # We need to create associated mentions inline because they need to exist _before_ creating any
68:       # other Article-related notifications. This ensures that users will not receive a second notification for the
69:       # post being published if they have already received an initial notification about being @-mentioned in the post.
70:       Mentions::CreateAll.call(notifiable)
71: 
72:       # Kicks off a worker to send any notifications about the post being published, if necessary.
73:       Notification.send_to_followers(notifiable, "Published")
74:     end
75: 
76:     def send_to_followers(notifiable, action = nil)
77:       Notifications::NotifiableActionWorker.perform_async(notifiable.id, notifiable.class.name, action)
78:     end
79: 
80:     def send_new_comment_notifications_without_delay(comment)
81:       return if comment.commentable_type == "PodcastEpisode"
82:       return if UserBlock.blocking?(comment.commentable.user_id, comment.user_id)
83: 
84:       Notifications::NewComment::Send.call(comment)
85:     end

87:     def send_new_badge_achievement_notification(badge_achievement)

89:     end
90: 
91:     def send_reaction_notification(reaction, receiver)
92:       return if reaction.skip_notification_for?(receiver)
93:       return if UserBlock.blocking?(receiver, reaction.user_id)
94: 
95:       Notifications::NewReactionWorker.perform_async(*reaction_notification_attributes(reaction, receiver))
96:     end
97: 
98:     def send_reaction_notification_without_delay(reaction, receiver)
99:       return if reaction.skip_notification_for?(receiver)
100:       return if UserBlock.blocking?(receiver, reaction.user_id)
101: 
102:       Notifications::NewReactionWorker.new.perform(*reaction_notification_attributes(reaction, receiver))
103:     end
104: 
105:     def send_mention_notification(mention)
106:       return if MentionDecorator.new(mention).mentioned_by_blocked_user?
107: 
108:       Notifications::MentionWorker.perform_async(mention.id)
109:     end

114:       Notifications::NewMention::Send.call(mention) if mention

121:     def send_moderation_notification(notifiable)
122:       return unless [Comment, Article].include?(notifiable.class)
123: 
124:       if notifiable.instance_of?(Comment) && UserBlock.blocking?(notifiable.commentable.user_id, notifiable.user_id)
125:         return
126:       end
127: 
128:       Notifications::CreateRoundRobinModerationNotificationsWorker.perform_async(notifiable.id, notifiable.class.to_s)
129:     end

134: 
135:     def send_milestone_notification(type:, article_id:)
136:       Notifications::MilestoneWorker.perform_async(type, article_id)
137:     end
138: 
139:     def remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids:, notifiable_type:, action: nil)
140:       return unless %w[Article Comment Mention].include?(notifiable_type) && notifiable_ids.present?
141: 
142:       Notifications::RemoveAllByAction.call(Array.wrap(notifiable_ids), notifiable_type, action)
143:     end

152:       return unless %w[Article Comment Mention].include?(notifiable_type) && notifiable_ids.present?
153: 
154:       Notifications::RemoveAllWorker.new.perform(notifiable_ids, notifiable_type)

156: 
157:     def update_notifications(notifiable, action = nil)
158:       Notifications::UpdateWorker.perform_async(notifiable.id, notifiable.class.name, action)
159:     end
160: 
161:     def fast_destroy_old_notifications(destroy_before_timestamp = ENV.fetch("FAST_DESTROY_OLD_NOTIFICATIONS_DAYS", 85).to_i.days.ago)
162:       return if destroy_before_timestamp > 7.days.ago
163: 
164:       sql = <<-SQL.squish
165:         DELETE FROM notifications
166:         WHERE notifications.id IN (
167:           SELECT notifications.id
168:           FROM notifications
169:           WHERE created_at < ?
170:           LIMIT 50000
171:         )
172:       SQL
173: 
174:       notification_sql = Notification.sanitize_sql([sql, destroy_before_timestamp])
175: 
176:       BulkSqlDelete.delete_in_batches(notification_sql)
177:     end
178: 
179:     def fast_cleanup_older_than_100_for(user_id)
180:       comment_sql = <<-SQL.squish
181:         DELETE FROM notifications
182:         WHERE notifications.id IN (
183:           SELECT id FROM (
184:             SELECT id FROM notifications
185:             WHERE user_id = ? AND notifiable_type = 'Comment'
186:             ORDER BY created_at DESC
187:             OFFSET 100
188:             LIMIT 1000
189:           ) as sub
190:           LIMIT 50000
191:         )
192:       SQL
193: 
194:       non_comment_sql = <<-SQL.squish
195:         DELETE FROM notifications
196:         WHERE notifications.id IN (
197:           SELECT id FROM (
198:             SELECT id FROM notifications
199:             WHERE user_id = ? AND (notifiable_type != 'Comment' OR notifiable_type IS NULL)
200:             ORDER BY created_at DESC
201:             OFFSET 100
202:             LIMIT 1000
203:           ) as sub
204:           LIMIT 50000
205:         )
206:       SQL
207: 
208:       BulkSqlDelete.delete_in_batches(Notification.sanitize_sql([comment_sql, user_id]))
209:       BulkSqlDelete.delete_in_batches(Notification.sanitize_sql([non_comment_sql, user_id]))
210:     end
211:

215:       reactable_data = Notifications::Reactions::ReactionData.coerce(reaction).to_h
216:       receiver_data = { "klass" => receiver.class.name, "id" => receiver.id }
217:       [reactable_data, receiver_data]
218:     end
219:   end

237: 
238:     Notifications::CleanupUserWorker.perform_async(user_id)
